body.title: [escalated] Granny stale-in_progress reset only covers CC-workers — a stuck builder ticket never auto-recovers
body.description:
**Defense-in-depth follow-up to the handshake-race fix (e60e9a13).** The race that ghost-stuck a builder ticket in in_progress is now fixed at the source, but the SAFETY NET is still builder-blind: _reset_stale_inprogress (daemon.py ~640) resets only CC-WORKER tickets stale past _STALE_INPROGRESS_TIMEOUT_S (2h). So if a builder ticket sticks in in_progress for ANY reason, _worker_busy stays True forever and the drain dies with no auto-recovery (I had to unstick T-consequence-model by hand). 

**Fix:** extend _reset_stale_inprogress to also reset stuck BUILDER-worker tickets (aider/dicksimnel) past the timeout — reset to sprint (re-dispatchable) and clear the worker so the drain self-heals. Consider a shorter timeout for builders (a build is minutes, not hours). 

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/granny/daemon.py (_reset_stale_inprogress). 
**Completion test:** a builder ticket stuck in_progress past the (builder) timeout is reset to sprint and the worker freed; CC-worker behavior unchanged. 
**Scope boundary:** the stale-in_progress safety net for builders. NOT the handshake race (fixed). 
**intention:** I intend that a stuck builder ticket self-heals so no single stuck ticket can silently kill the autonomous drain.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

## Escalation summary (Aider)
**What was tried:** aider TIMED OUT after 900s
**Where it broke:** no test target — correctness unverified (CC to validate)
**What now?**
